MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-08-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Ryan Vlasak, Esq. of Bradshaw & Assoc., 415-433-4800 for plaintiff and moving party
Bradshaw & Assoc.
Ann Mcfarland Draper, Esq.and Eric Farber, Esq. of Farber & Co., 415-434-5320 for defendant and opposing party Fern Isaacson

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows --
PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN. EVIDENCE SUPPORTING MOTION IS INADMISSABLE AND PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXISTS RE EXISTENCE FOR SERVICES RENDERED. THE COURT DENIED DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS PER CCP 437 c(g) and CCP 128.6. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (301/HEK) Judge Harold Kahn, Reporter LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Motion To Compel Attendance Of Defts At Deposition; Increase Deposition Limits; Responses To Form Interrogatories; Responses To Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-21-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Nicole M. Dabbous, for Plaintiff/moving party
No appearance for opposing party
Ruling:
Hearing held and Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion For Order To Compel Attendance Of Defendants At Deposition, To Increase Deposition Limits, To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, To Compel Responses To Requests For Production of Documents is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as it seeks responses to Form Interrogatories and Requests For Production of Documents. Responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
As the motion seeks to compel the depositions of Defendant Isaacson and Youell in San Francisco, the motion is denied. The depositions shall be noticed for Los Angeles. Counsel shall meet and confer to agree upon dates and location. Defendant shall agree upon dates no later than July 11, 2005 for the depositions.
The court finds that the failure to respond to the interrogatories and requests for documents and the failure to inform counsel of their relocation to Los Angeles were misuses of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defendants in the amount of $183.30, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Attorney's fees are not awarded as Plaintiff is acting in pro per. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
